title: Kim Jong Un Inspects Munitions Factories, Calls for Stronger Mortar and Howitzer Forces
summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un visited multiple munitions factories on May 11 to review weapons production, calling for strengthened mortar and howitzer capabilities and ordering a new specialized artillery production complex.
contents:
## Kim Jong Un Munitions Factory Inspection

### Factory Visit Details

North Korean leader Kim Jong Un visited several munitions industrial enterprises on May 11, 2026, to review the implementation of the nation's weapons production plan for the first half of the year, according to the Korean Central News Agency (KCNA) report published May 13.

### Artillery Buildup

Kim repeatedly stressed the need to strengthen mortar and howitzer forces during the inspection tour. He ordered the establishment of a specialized artillery production complex and a small-arms production factory to meet the military's future demands. At a bullet production facility, Kim set forth tasks for establishing a production system for high-precision multipurpose bullets of different calibers.

### Production Achievements

KCNA reported that Kim praised workers in the munitions sector for achieving record growth in shell and bullet production. He emphasized modernizing North Korea's munitions industry through upgrades to production structures, technical control systems, and quality inspection mechanisms.

### Senior Officials Present

Key defense officials accompanied Kim, including Jo Chun-ryong, a party secretary, and Kim Jong-sik, first vice department director of the party's Central Committee. Pak Jong-chon, adviser to the Ministry of National Defence, also attended.

### Strategic Context

The inspection follows Kim's earlier visit to a munitions factory on May 6, where he revealed plans to deploy a new 155mm self-propelled gun-howitzer with a striking range exceeding 60 kilometers along the southern border by year's end. North Korea continues to accelerate military buildup under its five-year defense development plan unveiled at the 9th Workers' Party Congress in February 2026.

title: Ship Seized Near UAE Coast and Redirected Toward Iran as Hormuz Tensions Escalate
summary: A vessel anchored off the UAE port of Fujairah has been seized by unauthorized personnel and redirected toward Iranian waters, while an Indian cargo ship sank off Oman after a suspected drone or missile attack.
contents:
## Strait of Hormuz Maritime Incidents

### Vessel Seizure Near Fujairah

The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) reported on May 14, 2026, that unauthorized personnel boarded a vessel anchored approximately 38 nautical miles northeast of the UAE port of Fujairah. The ship now heads toward Iranian territorial waters, according to British military authorities.

### Indian Cargo Ship Sunk

Separately, the Indian-flagged cargo vessel Haji Ali sank off the coast of Oman on May 14 following a suspected explosion attributed to a drone or missile strike, according to maritime security firm Vanguard. All 14 Indian crew members reached safety after rescue by Oman's coast guard. India's Ministry of External Affairs condemned the attack as unacceptable.

### Floating Armoury Seized

Maritime risk company Vanguard also identified the Honduras-flagged Hui Chuan, reportedly operating as a floating armoury storing weapons for maritime security firms, among vessels seized in the area.

### Chinese Ships Transit Hormuz

Iranian semi-official news agencies reported that China received permission to transit several vessels through the strait under new Iranian protocols, following requests from China's foreign minister and Beijing's ambassador to Iran. A Chinese supertanker carrying 2 million barrels of Iraqi crude also sailed through the strait on May 14.

### Diplomatic Context

The incidents coincided with the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing, where both leaders agreed that the Strait of Hormuz must remain open for the free flow of energy. Iran's senior vice president Mohammadreza Aref declared that the strait belongs to Iran and Tehran would not relinquish control at any price.

title: Trump-Xi Beijing Summit Addresses Iran War, Hormuz Crisis, and Taiwan Amid Escalating Global Tensions
summary: US President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping held a two-hour summit in Beijing discussing Iran nuclear concerns, Strait of Hormuz reopening, and Taiwan, with both sides agreeing on key principles while major disagreements persist.
contents:
## Trump-Xi Beijing Summit Security Assessment

### Summit Overview

US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping conducted a two-hour meeting at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on May 14, 2026, marking Trump's first visit to China since returning to the White House. The summit addressed multiple critical security flashpoints simultaneously unfolding across the Indo-Pacific and Middle East regions.

### Iran and Hormuz Agreements

The White House confirmed that both leaders agreed the Strait of Hormuz must remain open to support the free flow of energy, and that Iran can never acquire nuclear weapons. Xi made clear China's opposition to the militarization of the strait. US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told CNBC he expected China would do what they can to help reopen the waterway, characterizing it as very much in Beijing's interest.

### Taiwan Tensions

Xi told Trump that the Taiwan question represents the most important issue in China-US relations, warning that mishandling the matter could send relations down a dangerous path. Xi described Taiwan independence and cross-strait peace as irreconcilable as fire and water. Taiwan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs responded by calling Beijing the sole risk to regional peace and stability.

### Concurrent Military Activity

Taiwan's Ministry of National Defence detected 3 PLA aircraft sorties and 6 PLAN vessels operating around its territory on May 14, with all three sorties entering Taiwan's southwestern and eastern ADIZ. This military activity continued during the summit, underscoring Beijing's dual-track approach of diplomacy paired with military pressure.

### Risk Assessment

The summit produced agreements on broad principles but left fundamental disputes unresolved. The simultaneous Hormuz seizures, Iran's defiant posture, and continued PLA activity around Taiwan demonstrate that multiple security crises continue to evolve in parallel, each capable of rapid escalation.

title: US Treasury Sanctions Nine Companies and Three Individuals Over Iran Oil Shipments to China
summary: The US Treasury's OFAC designated nine companies in Hong Kong, UAE, and Oman, plus three IRGC-linked individuals, for facilitating Iranian oil shipments to China, as Treasury Secretary Bessent presses Beijing to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
contents:
## US Treasury Iran Oil Sanctions

### New Designations

The US Department of Treasury announced on May 11, 2026, sanctions against three individuals and nine companies for aiding Iran's shipment of oil to China. The Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) designations targeted entities that helped Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) sell and ship its allotment of Iranian oil through a network of front companies.

### Targeted Entities

The sanctioned companies span multiple jurisdictions: four Hong Kong-based firms (Hong Kong Blue Ocean Ltd, Hong Kong Sanmu Ltd, Jiandi HK Ltd, Max Honor International Trade Co Ltd), four UAE-based firms (Ocean Allianz Shipping LLC, Atic Energy FZE, Blanca Goods Wholesaler LLC, Universal Fortune Trading LLC), and one Oman-based entity (Zeus Logistics Group). The three sanctioned individuals work for the IRGC's Shahid Purja'fari oil headquarters.

### Treasury Secretary Statement

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent declared that the administration would continue using sanctions to deprive Iran's government and military of funding for weapons, its nuclear program, and support for regional proxies. Bessent accused China of indirectly financing Iran by purchasing 90 percent of Iranian energy exports.

### State Department Reward

The State Department simultaneously announced a reward of up to $15 million for information leading to the disruption of the IRGC's financial mechanisms.

### Strategic Context

The sanctions build on previous measures imposed in July 2025 targeting Turkey-based Golden Globe, which handles hundreds of millions of dollars in IRGC oil sales annually. The announcement came days before Trump's Beijing summit with Xi Jinping, where reopening the Strait of Hormuz and constraining Iran's nuclear program dominated the agenda.

title: AFP Monitors 35 Chinese Vessels Across Key West Philippine Sea Features During Balikatan 2026
summary: The Armed Forces of the Philippines reported 35 Chinese vessels from the People's Liberation Army Navy and China Coast Guard monitored across Ayungin Shoal, Bajo de Masinloc, Escoda Shoal, and Pag-asa Islands from May 4-11 during the Balikatan 2026 exercises.
contents:
## Chinese Vessel Activity in West Philippine Sea

### AFP Monitoring Report

The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) disclosed on May 12, 2026, that a total of 35 Chinese vessels operated near key features in the West Philippine Sea from May 4 to 11. AFP spokesperson Rear Admiral Roy Vincent Trinidad confirmed the vessel count during a press briefing at Camp Aguinaldo.

### Vessel Distribution

The Chinese presence included vessels from both the People's Liberation Army Navy (PLAN) and China Coast Guard (CCG) distributed across four strategic features:
- Bajo de Masinloc (Scarborough Shoal): 17 vessels (8 PLAN, 9 CCG)
- Ayungin Shoal (Second Thomas Shoal): 7 vessels (1 PLAN, 6 CCG)
- Pag-asa Islands (Thitu Island): 6 vessels (3 PLAN, 3 CCG)
- Escoda Shoal (Sabina Shoal): 5 vessels (3 PLAN, 2 CCG)

### Balikatan Context

The monitoring period coincided with the Balikatan 2026 joint military exercises between the Philippines and the United States, which ran from April 20 to May 8. Allied nations including Japan, Australia, and Canada also participated. Trinidad noted that no aggressive or coercive actions from Chinese vessels occurred during the exercises.

### Research Vessel Concerns

Separately, the PCG continued tracking the Chinese oceanographic research vessel Xiang Yang Hong 33, which conducted survey activities across multiple Philippine-claimed features since mid-April. The PCG also detected Chinese Coast Guard vessel BN 5309 and 13 Chinese maritime militia vessels near Rozul Reef, with 28 more militia vessels clustered around Pag-asa Island.

### Trend Analysis

The 35-vessel count represents a decrease from the 62 Chinese vessels spotted in the same areas during April. However, the persistent presence of both military and coast guard vessels underscores Beijing's continued assertion of its contested claims in the strategic waterway.

title: Indian Cargo Ship Haji Ali Sinks Off Oman Coast After Suspected Drone or Missile Strike
summary: The Indian-flagged cargo vessel Haji Ali sank off the coast of Oman on May 14 following a suspected drone or missile attack while en route from Somalia to the UAE, with all 14 crew members rescued by Oman's coast guard.
contents:
## Indian Cargo Vessel Sunk Off Oman

### Attack Details

The Indian-flagged cargo ship Haji Ali sank off the coast of Oman on May 14, 2026, following what maritime security firm Vanguard assessed as a suspected drone or missile strike. The vessel, a 57-meter ship, had departed Berbera Port in Somalia on May 6 carrying livestock bound for Sharjah in the UAE.

### Crew Rescue

All 14 Indian crew members abandoned ship after an explosion sparked a fire aboard the vessel. Oman Coast Guard units rescued the crew and transferred them to Diba Port. India's Ministry of External Affairs confirmed the safety of all crew members and condemned the attack as unacceptable.

### Concurrent Seizure Incident

The sinking occurred alongside a separate incident in which unauthorized personnel boarded a vessel anchored 38 nautical miles northeast of Fujairah, UAE, and redirected it toward Iranian territorial waters. The UK Maritime Trade Operations agency reported the seizure and urged vessels in the area to report suspicious activity.

### Iran Claims Strait Authority

Iran's judiciary spokesperson Asghar Jahangir declared on May 14 that Iran holds legal and judicial authority to seize oil tankers connected to the US in the Strait of Hormuz, citing alleged violations of international maritime law. Iran's senior vice president Mohammadreza Aref asserted that the strait has always belonged to Iran.

### Maritime Shipping Impact

These incidents further degrade commercial shipping confidence in the region. The Strait of Hormuz, through which one-fifth of global oil previously transited, remains effectively contested since the US-Iran conflict escalated in late February 2026. Insurance premiums and shipping costs continue to climb as attacks persist despite diplomatic efforts.

title: Xi Jinping Warns Trump That Taiwan Dispute Could Trigger US-China Conflict
summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping told US President Trump during their Beijing summit that disagreement over Taiwan could send bilateral relations down a dangerous path, calling Taiwan independence and cross-strait peace irreconcilable as fire and water.
contents:
## Xi's Taiwan Warning at Beijing Summit

### Summit Confrontation on Taiwan

Chinese President Xi Jinping delivered a pointed warning to US President Donald Trump during their two-hour meeting at the Great Hall of the People on May 14, 2026, declaring that Taiwan represents the most important issue in China-US relations. According to Xinhua News Agency, Xi told Trump that proper handling would ensure overall bilateral stability, while mismanagement would lead to clashes and even conflicts.

### Taiwan Independence Red Line

Xi described Taiwan independence and cross-strait peace as irreconcilable as fire and water, reinforcing Beijing's long-standing position that any moves toward formal Taiwanese sovereignty would trigger a forceful response. The warning came as Taiwan's opposition-controlled parliament recently approved only two-thirds of a US$40 billion special defense budget, cutting domestic programs including drone development.

### Taiwan's Response

Taiwan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs fired back, declaring Beijing the sole risk to regional peace and stability. MOFA emphasized that Taiwan (Republic of China) and the People's Republic of China remain not subordinate to each other, and that Beijing holds no right to represent Taiwan internationally. Cabinet spokesperson Michelle Lee said any actions contributing to regional stability should receive positive assessment.

### Defense Budget Dispute

Taiwan's government announced on May 14 that it considers resubmitting to parliament the defense items cut from the special budget, citing their critical importance to national security. Premier Cho Jung-tai directed the defense ministry to explore options including a supplementary budget. A senior US official expressed disappointment at the reduced spending level.

### PLA Military Activity

Meanwhile, Taiwan's Ministry of National Defence detected 3 PLA aircraft sorties and 6 PLAN vessels operating around its territory on the day of the summit. All three aircraft sorties entered Taiwan's southwestern and eastern ADIZ, continuing the gray zone pressure campaign that Beijing has maintained since September 2020.

title: Taiwan Detects 3 PLA Aircraft and 6 PLAN Vessels as Government Moves to Restore Cut Defense Budget
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defence detected 3 PLA aircraft sorties entering its ADIZ and 6 PLAN vessels on May 14, while the government announced plans to resubmit defense budget items cut by the opposition-controlled parliament.
contents:
## Taiwan Defense Developments

### PLA Activity Detection

Taiwan's Ministry of National Defence (MND) detected 3 sorties of People's Liberation Army aircraft and 6 PLAN vessels operating around its territory as of 6 AM (UTC+8) on May 14, 2026. All three sorties entered Taiwan's southwestern and eastern Air Defence Identification Zone (ADIZ). The ROC Armed Forces monitored the situation and responded with mission aircraft, naval vessels, and shore-based missile systems.

### Escalation Trend

The May 14 detections followed a May 13 report of 2 PLA aircraft sorties, 7 PLAN vessels, and 1 official ship, with 1 sortie entering the southwestern ADIZ. On May 12, the MND recorded 9 PLA aircraft sorties, with 5 entering the southwestern and eastern ADIZ. This pattern of sustained gray zone operations continues the pressure campaign Beijing has maintained since September 2020.

### Defense Budget Restoration Effort

Taiwan's Executive Yuan announced on May 14 that Premier Cho Jung-tai directed the Ministry of National Defense to explore resubmitting budget items cut from the special defense budget. The opposition-controlled Legislative Yuan last week approved only two-thirds of the requested US$40 billion special defense budget, funding US arms purchases but cutting domestic programs including drones and anti-ballistic missile systems.

### US Concern

A senior US official expressed disappointment at the reduced defense spending on May 10, saying it fell short of what Washington considers necessary. The timing of the budget controversy proves especially sensitive given Trump's concurrent visit to Beijing, where his administration has strongly supported Taiwan's military modernization.

### MOFA Response to Xi

Cabinet spokesperson Michelle Lee declared that continuously strengthening national defence and effective collective deterrence remains the single most critical factor in ensuring regional peace and stability, directly responding to Xi Jinping's warning to Trump that the Taiwan question could destabilize US-China relations.

title: Russia-North Korea Military Alliance Deepens as Constitutional Amendments Formalize Nuclear Command Structure
summary: North Korea's revised constitution codifies a decentralized nuclear command-and-control structure and abandons reunification goals, while Russia-DPRK military cooperation expands with a five-year plan and North Korean soldiers marching in Russia's Victory Day Parade.
contents:
## Russia-DPRK Strategic Partnership Evolution

### Constitutional Nuclear Authority

North Korea's recently amended constitution, released by South Korea's Ministry of Unification on May 6, 2026, grants the chairman of the State Affairs Commission (SAC) explicit command authority over nuclear forces for the first time. Article 89 establishes Kim Jong Un's direct command and control, while also creating a mechanism for delegating nuclear launch authority to the Nuclear Forces Command under the SAC.

### Abandonment of Reunification

The constitution deleted all references to national reunification throughout the preamble, redefining North Korea's national mission as socialist construction rather than unification. The amendments defined North Korea's territory as bordering China and Russia to the north and South Korea to the south, formally establishing a two-state framework.

### Expanded Russia-DPRK Cooperation

North Korean soldiers participated in Russia's 81st Victory Day Parade on May 9, 2026, marking their first appearance in the event. Russian President Putin personally expressed gratitude to North Korean Commander Senior Colonel Choe Yong Hun, who analysts believe gained prominence through achievements in North Korea's operations in Kursk Oblast, Russia.

### Five-Year Military Plan

Russian Defense Minister Andrei Belousov met with Kim Jong Un on April 26 and announced plans for a five-year military cooperation plan. This builds on the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership signed in June 2024, which included a 20-year mutual defense obligation.

### Strategic Implications

The ISW-CDOT assessed that North Korea's constitutional changes target South Korea's Three-Axis System, designed to counter DPRK nuclear and missile threats. By codifying delegated nuclear command authority, Pyongyang aims to project credible retaliatory capabilities and weaken allied deterrence strategies based on offensive doctrine. Meanwhile, the deepening Russia-DPRK axis enables Pyongyang to diversify from its 98-percent trade dependence on China.

title: Philippine Coast Guard Deploys Aircraft to Challenge Chinese Research Vessel Conducting Unauthorized Survey Near Spratly Islands
summary: The PCG deployed aircraft and issued radio warnings to the Chinese research vessel Xiang Yang Hong 33 operating near Rozul Reef in the Spratly Islands, while detecting 41 Chinese maritime militia vessels swarming the area around Philippine-claimed features.
contents:
## PCG Challenges Chinese Research Operations

### Aircraft Deployment

The Philippine Coast Guard deployed aircraft on May 7, 2026, to issue radio warnings to the Chinese research vessel Xiang Yang Hong 33 operating near Rozul Reef in the contested Spratly Island chain. The vessel, capable of supporting submersible craft, had deployed a service boat toward Iroquois Reef, confirming ongoing unauthorized marine scientific research operations.

### Extensive Chinese Presence

During the PCG air patrol, 41 Chinese maritime militia vessels (CMMVs) appeared anchored in waters surrounding Iroquois Reef and Philippine-occupied Thitu Island (Pag-asa Island). China Coast Guard vessel 5101 maintained position approximately two nautical miles west of the island. A separate detection found Chinese Coast Guard vessel BN 5309 and 13 CMMVs near Rozul Reef.

### Research Vessel Tracking

The Xiang Yang Hong 33, detected using Canada's Dark Vessel Detection System, conducted survey activities across multiple Philippine-claimed features since departing China over three weeks earlier. Its track included stops near Ayungin Shoal (April 20-22), Escoda Shoal (April 22-28), Arellano Reef and Bulig Shoal (April 28-May 1), Panganiban Reef (May 1-3), and Quirino Atoll and Rozul Reef (May 3-6).

### Philippine Government Response

PCG Commandant Admiral Ronnie Gil Gavan declared that Beijing's activities constitute a clear and blatant violation of the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea (UNCLOS), emphasizing that the Philippines granted no consent for any marine scientific research activities in its waters.

### Civilian Mission Success

The Atin Ito coalition completed its fourth civilian mission to the West Philippine Sea, with Akbayan Representative Dadah Kiram Ismula planting a Philippine flag on Sandy Cay 1 near Pag-asa Island on May 3, despite the visible presence of Chinese Coast Guard vessels. Three Chinese boats attempted to pursue the convoy during the flag-planting operation.

title: BBC Verify Tracks 184 Sanctioned Russian Shadow Fleet Vessels Transiting UK Waters Since March
summary: BBC Verify analysis revealed that 184 UK-sanctioned Russian shadow fleet vessels made 238 journeys through UK waters since Prime Minister Starmer threatened interception in March, with no confirmed boarding operations conducted.
contents:
## Russian Shadow Fleet Defies UK Interception Threat

### Discovery

BBC Verify investigators tracked 184 UK-sanctioned vessels making 238 separate journeys through United Kingdom waters between March 25 and May 11, 2026, using MarineTraffic AIS data. The analysis revealed a stark gap between political rhetoric and maritime enforcement.

### Key Findings

- 184 sanctioned vessels completed 238 transits through UK Exclusive Economic Zone
- 94 instances involved crossings into UK territorial waters (12 nautical mile zone)
- 173 oil tankers, 10 LNG tankers, and 1 multipurpose offshore vessel comprised the fleet
- The sanctioned oil tanker Universal received escort from Russian frigate Admiral Grigorovich
- The Yi Tong, a Chinese-registered tanker, altered its route around Scotland to avoid the Channel

### Enforcement Gap

Former Royal Navy commander Tom Sharpe called the lack of action "pathetic" and "utterly confusing." The Ministry of Defence claimed "disrupting and deterring" operations against over 700 suspected vessels since October 2024, yet provided no evidence of any boardings.

### Legal Complexity

Shipping lawyer James Turner KC highlighted that international maritime law restricts seizing vessels flying another country's flag in transit, creating significant legal barriers to the UK's stated interception policy.

### Strategic Impact

The re-routing of vessels like Yi Tong around Scotland suggests the UK policy exerts some pressure, increasing fuel costs and transit times for shadow fleet operators moving sanctioned Russian oil.

title: Kim Jong Un Calls for Stronger Mortar and Howitzer Forces During Munitions Factory Inspections
summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un stressed the need to strengthen mortar and howitzer forces during inspections of multiple munitions factories, reviewing implementation of the first-half 2026 weapons production plan.
contents:
## Kim Jong Un Inspects Munitions Industrial Enterprises

### Factory Tour

KCNA reported on May 13 that Kim Jong Un visited several munitions industrial enterprises on May 11, reviewing the implementation of the country's weapons production plan for the first half of 2026. The visits continued an intensified military inspection campaign.

### Artillery Modernization

At a bullet production factory, Kim set forth tasks for establishing production systems for high-precision multipurpose bullets of different calibers. He repeatedly stressed the need to strengthen mortar and howitzer forces and ordered the establishment of a specialized artillery production complex and a small-arms production factory.

### Recent Military Activity Timeline

- May 6: Kim visited a major munitions factory, revealing plans to deploy a new 155mm self-propelled gun-howitzer along the southern border by year's end
- May 7: Kim boarded the 5,000-ton destroyer Choe Hyon and observed a maneuvering assessment test in the Yellow Sea
- May 11: Kim inspected multiple munitions factories and praised workers for achieving record growth in shell and bullet production

### Senior Officials Present

Kim brought key defense officials including Jo Chun-ryong (party secretary), Kim Jong-sik (first director of munitions industry department), and Pak Jong-chon (adviser to Ministry of National Defence).

### Strategic Context

The inspections align with North Korea's five-year defense development plan from the February 2026 Ninth Party Congress, which mandated reinforcing SRBM deployments and greatly increasing the density of concentrated attack capability against South Korea.

title: UANI Identifies 37 Iranian Oil-Laden Tankers Operating Inside Persian Gulf Amid US Blockade
summary: United Against Nuclear Iran tracking shows at least 37 tankers laden with Iranian oil operating inside the Persian Gulf, with Iran-flagged vessels employing Lombok Strait routing to evade US naval enforcement.
contents:
## Iranian Ghost Fleet Sustains Operations Under US Blockade

### Current Situation

UANI tracking as of May 11 identified at least 37 tankers laden with Iranian oil operating inside the Persian Gulf, west of the Strait of Hormuz. The US blockade, initiated April 13, targets vessels entering and exiting Iranian ports carrying sanctioned or contraband oil, but does not apply to all Iran-linked ships.

### Evasion Tactics Documented

Iran-flagged tankers have adopted new routing through the Lombok Strait in Indonesia rather than the traditional Malacca and Singapore Straits passage, taking uneconomical routes through the Indian Ocean to avoid US naval interdiction.

### Specific Vessel Movements

- HUGE (IMO 9357183): Transited Lombok Strait on May 3 carrying crude loaded at Kharg Island on March 31
- DERYA (IMO 9569700): Observed at Malaysian EOPL anchorage, likely conducting STS transfer operations
- TOUR 2 (IMO 9364112): Departed Malaysian EOPL on May 11, broadcasting AIS while transiting South China Sea northbound

### Scale of Operations

Since February 28, UANI tracked at least 70 Iranian oil loadings (41 from Kharg Island), representing approximately 82 million barrels generating an estimated $6 billion in revenue for the IRGC. At least 42 STS transfers of Iranian oil occurred at the Malaysian EOPL anchorage.

### US Enforcement Update

US CENTCOM reported redirecting 62 commercial ships and disabling 4 vessels since the blockade commenced. Open transits through the Strait have fallen to near zero vessels per day since May 6.

title: PLA Conducts ADIZ Incursions Near Taiwan as Trump-Xi Summit Opens in Beijing
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense detected 3 PLA aircraft sorties and 6 PLAN vessels operating around Taiwan on May 14, with all three sorties entering the southwestern and eastern ADIZ during the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing.
contents:
## PLA Activity Around Taiwan During Trump-Xi Summit

### Detection Report

Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense reported detecting 3 PLA aircraft sorties and 6 PLAN vessels operating around Taiwan on May 14, up until 6:00 AM UTC+8. All three sorties entered Taiwan's southwestern and eastern Air Defence Identification Zone.

### Summit Context

The PLA activity coincided with President Trump's arrival in Beijing for a summit with Xi Jinping. Xi told Trump that the Taiwan question represents the "most important issue" in US-China relations and warned that mishandling could lead to "clashes and even conflicts."

### Recent Activity Pattern

- May 12: 9 PLA sorties, 7 PLAN vessels, 1 official ship; 5 sorties entered ADIZ
- May 13: 2 PLA sorties, 7 PLAN vessels, 1 official ship; 1 sortie entered ADIZ
- May 14: 3 PLA sorties, 6 PLAN vessels; all 3 entered ADIZ

### Taiwan Defense Response

ROC Armed Forces deployed aircraft, naval ships, and coastal-based missile systems to monitor the situation. Since September 2020, China has incrementally increased gray zone activities around Taiwan.

### Arms Sales Dimension

A $14 billion US arms package awaits Trump's approval, with China reiterating strong opposition to the sales. Xi raised the issue directly during the summit, linking it to the overall stability of bilateral relations.

title: Taiwan MND Reports All PLA Sorties Penetrated ADIZ on Eve of Trump-Xi Taiwan Discussion
summary: Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense confirmed 3 out of 3 PLA aircraft sorties entered the southwestern and eastern ADIZ on May 14, marking a 100% penetration rate as Taiwan's defense concerns dominated the Trump-Xi summit agenda.
contents:
## Taiwan Defense Ministry ADIZ Penetration Report

### Official Statement

The Ministry of National Defense posted on X: "3 sorties of PLA aircraft and 6 PLAN vessels operating around Taiwan detected up until 6 a.m. (UTC+8) today. 3 out of 3 sorties entered Taiwan's southwestern and eastern part ADIZ. ROC Armed Forces have monitored the situation and responded."

### Operational Response

Taiwan deployed aircraft, naval ships, and activated coastal-based missile systems to track and monitor the incursions. The response followed standard operating procedures developed since China's gray zone campaign intensified after September 2020.

### Defense Budget Implications

Taiwan's Legislative Yuan remains gridlocked over a special defense budget bill. The DPP-backed version allocates nearly $40 billion USD for asymmetric capabilities, while the KMT proposal caps at roughly $12 billion USD. US Indo-Pacific Commander Admiral Paparo has urged rapid passage of the budget.

### Trump-Xi Dynamic

The White House readout of the Trump-Xi meeting notably omitted any mention of Taiwan, while Xinhua's Chinese readout prominently featured Xi's warnings about the island. This divergence signals both sides' sensitivity around the topic.

### Monthly Statistics

April 2026 recorded 187 PLA aircraft contacts and 207 PLAN ship contacts around Taiwan, continuing the sustained pressure campaign that has normalized near-daily military presence in waters and airspace surrounding the island.

title: Trump and Xi Agree Iran Cannot Have Nuclear Weapons and Strait of Hormuz Must Remain Open
summary: Presidents Trump and Xi concluded a 135-minute summit in Beijing, agreeing that Iran can never acquire nuclear weapons and that the Strait of Hormuz must remain open, while Xi warned of potential conflicts over Taiwan.
contents:
## Trump-Xi Beijing Summit Produces Agreement on Iran and Hormuz

### Summit Outcomes

US President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping held a 135-minute meeting at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on May 14, producing several agreements on pressing global issues.

### Key Agreements

- Both sides affirmed that the Strait of Hormuz must remain open to support free energy flow
- Xi made clear China's opposition to militarization of the strait and any effort to charge tolls
- Xi expressed interest in purchasing more American oil to reduce Chinese dependence on the strait
- Both countries agreed that Iran can never possess a nuclear weapon
- The leaders discussed expanding market access for American businesses into China

### Business Delegation

Trump brought prominent US business leaders including Elon Musk (Tesla/SpaceX), Jensen Huang (Nvidia), Tim Cook (Apple), Kelly Ortberg (Boeing), and Sanjay Mehrotra (Micron Technology). The executives joined a portion of the bilateral meeting.

### Taiwan Friction

Xi told Trump that the Taiwan question represents the most important issue in US-China relations, warning that mishandling could trigger direct confrontation. The White House readout notably excluded any mention of Taiwan.

### Next Steps

Trump invited Xi for a reciprocal White House visit on September 24. Additional meetings will take place at the APEC summit in Shenzhen (November) and G20 in Florida (December).

title: UN Demands Probe Into Nigerian Zamfara Market Airstrike as Military Denies Civilian Deaths
summary: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Turk demanded independent investigations into Nigerian military airstrikes on a market in Zamfara State that Amnesty International reports killed at least 100 civilians on May 10.
contents:
## UN Calls for Investigation Into Nigerian Airstrike on Zamfara Market

### The Incident

Amnesty International's Nigeria chapter reported that Nigerian military airstrikes on a crowded market in Tumfa Village, Shinkafi Local Government Area of Zamfara State, killed at least 100 civilians on May 10-11, 2026. A resident of a nearby village put the death toll at 117.

### International Response

UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Turk stated he found the reports shocking and demanded prompt, thorough, independent, and impartial investigations. He also expressed alarm at reports of high civilian casualties from Chadian fighter jet strikes against Boko Haram camps on Lake Chad islands, which reportedly killed dozens of Nigerian fishermen.

### Nigerian Military Denial

The Defence Headquarters denied civilian casualties, stating the operation targeted a "confirmed high-level gathering of terrorist ring leaders" at a location verified by multi-source human intelligence. Major General Michael Onoja stated varying casualty figures in circulation remain "speculative, unverified" and do not represent the official position.

### Pattern of Concern

The National Human Rights Commission cited recent incidents in Jilli Market (Yobe State), Shiroro Market (Niger State), and Tumfa Market (Zamfara State) within a two-month span, warning that repeated bombardments of communities and marketplaces erode public trust.

### Turk's Broader Demands

The UN rights chief urged both Nigerian and Chadian militaries to take all feasible precautions to avoid harming civilians and to conduct operations in full compliance with international humanitarian law.

title: Xi Offers to Buy More US Oil at Beijing Summit as China Faces Hormuz Energy Supply Disruption
summary: Chinese President Xi expressed interest in purchasing more American oil during the Trump-Xi summit, signaling Beijing's concern over energy supply vulnerability as the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed to commercial traffic.
contents:
## China Seeks Alternative Oil Supply as Hormuz Disruption Threatens Energy Security

### Summit Energy Discussion

During the May 14 Beijing summit, Xi Jinping expressed interest in purchasing more American oil to reduce China's dependence on the Strait of Hormuz, according to a White House readout. US Treasury Secretary Bessent noted that China obtains roughly a third of its energy needs from the Gulf.

### China's Energy Vulnerability

China imports approximately 45-50% of its oil from the Middle East, with the Hormuz Strait serving as the critical chokepoint. Chinese net seaborne oil imports have fallen from 14 million barrels per day a year ago to 8.5 million barrels per day currently, according to Marex data.

### Iranian Oil Dependence

China purchases roughly 80-90% of Iran's crude exports, making it Tehran's most important economic lifeline. Iranian oil represented about 10% of China's total energy imports before the conflict. Chinese Commerce Ministry recently ordered domestic refiners not to comply with new US sanctions targeting processors of Iranian crude.

### Strategic Reserve Buffer

China has spent the past two years purchasing far more oil than domestic need, building strong strategic reserves that provide a buffer against supply disruption. Beijing has also accelerated development of alternative energy sources.

### Market Impact

Brent crude futures have surged roughly 50% since the conflict started, with international benchmark trading around $105-106. The oil price shock has contributed to inflation pressures globally, particularly affecting the US ahead of midterm elections.

links.label: Yahoo Finance - Trump-Xi Summit Iran Analysis

title: Sudanese Army Intelligence Sting Operations Drive Senior RSF Commander Defections
summary: Brigadier Ali Rizqallah became the third senior RSF commander to defect to the Sudanese military since 2024, with experts revealing army intelligence sting operations and financial incentives behind the defections.
contents:
## Army Intelligence Operations Behind RSF Commander Defections in Sudan

### Latest Defection

Brigadier Ali Rizqallah, known by his nickname Savanah, confirmed his defection to the Sudanese military on May 11, becoming the third senior RSF commander to switch sides since 2024. Speculation had circulated for nearly two weeks before confirmation.

### Intelligence Operations Revealed

US-based Sudanese expert Sami Saeed told The National that the defections result from "patient and carefully planned sting operations by military intelligence operatives," combined with monetary incentives to lure commanders away from the Rapid Support Forces.

### Previous High-Profile Defectors

- Abu Aqla Kaikal (October 2024): The first senior RSF commander to defect, he played a vital role in the army's campaign to recapture central Sudan and Khartoum
- General Al Nour Ahmed Adam (Al Qouba): Met General Al Burhan in Donqola this month; army drones cleared RSF checkpoints along his escape route from Darfur

### RSF Strength Persists

Experts caution against overstating the defections' impact. The RSF controls the entire Darfur region (roughly the size of France) and maintains presence across Kordofan and Blue Nile state. The paramilitary's long-range drone arsenal allows strikes reaching Port Sudan on the Red Sea.

### Humanitarian Crisis

The war, which erupted in April 2023, has killed tens of thousands and displaced at least 12 million people. More than half of Sudan's population — 25 million people — faces hunger.
